# Results

We were able to successfully estimate apparent survival and abundance for both *Elliptio 
* and *Alasmidonta varicosa*. 
We were unable to estimate these parameters for either *Elliptio fisheriana* or *Lampsilis cardium*. 
No models for these species successfully converged, most likely due to low initial releases and recapture rates.
For estimation of *E. complanata*, and *A. varicosa*, we used the model where apparent survival varied by by facility but the same between sampling occasions, and capture probability varied by time but the same for both facilities.
Apparent survival estimates ranged from `{r} min_survival_varicosa` to `{r} max_survival_varicosa` for *A. varicosa* and from `{r} min_survival_complanata` to `{r} max_survival_complanata` for *E. complanata* (@fig-survival). 
Survival was higher for Harrison Lake releases for both species, although the difference was much greater for *A. varicosa*. 

Abundance estimates of the uniquely tagged initial release cohorts ranged from `{r} min_abundance_varicosa` to `{r} max_abundance_varicosa` for *A. varicosa* and `{r} min_abundance_complanata` to `{r} max_abundance_complanata` for *E. complanata* (@tbl-abundance). 
These estimates were directly derived from the initial release of uniquely tagged mussels and survival estimates.
Similar to apparent survival, abundance estimates were higher for Harrison Lake releases for both species, 
This difference was less pronounced when reporting abundance estimates as a percentage of the initial release (@fig-abundance-percent).
Abundance estimates of the uniquely tagged releases of FMCC were substantially lower than Harrison Lake.
However, FMCC released a substantial number of non-uniquely tagged and untagged mussels. 
Applying the estimated survival to the entire release cohort of FMCC mussels results in abundance estimates ranging from `{r} min_abundance_all_varicosa` to `{r} max_abundance_all_varicosa` for *A. varicosa* and `{r} min_abundance_all_complanata` to `{r} max_abundance_all_complanata` for *E. complanata* (@tbl-abundance_all and @fig-abundance-all).  

# Figures

![Apparent survival estimates from the selected POPAN model. Error bars indicate 95% CIs. Each row (i.e., species) represents results from a single analysis/model.](../../results/figures/07_reduced_model_results/Figure_1_survival.jpg){#fig-survival}

![Abundance estimates from the selected POPAN model, reported as a percentage of the initial release of uniquely tagged individuals. Error bars indicate 95% CIs. Each row (i.e., species) represents results from a single analysis/model.](../../results/figures/07_reduced_model_results/Figure_2_abundance_percent_release.jpg){#fig-abundance-percent}

![Abundance estimates of all individuals from the initial release (uniquely tagged, non-uniquely tagged, and untagged). Based on apparent survival estimates from the selected POPAN model and initial number of mussels released. Error bars indicate 95% CIs. Each row (i.e., species) represents results from a single analysis/model.](../../results/figures/07_reduced_model_results/Figure_3_abundance_full_cohort.jpg){#fig-abundance-all}

# Tables

```{r}
#| label: tbl-abundance
#| tbl-cap: 'Abundance estimates from the selected POPAN model. Each row (i.e., species) represents results from a single analysis/model.'
make_abundance_table(tbl_abundance_summary)

```
```{r}
#| label: tbl-abundance_all
#| tbl-cap: 'Abundance estimates of all individuals from the initial release (uniquely tagged, non-uniquely tagged, and untagged). Based on apparent survival estimates from the selected POPAN model and initial number of mussels released. Each row (i.e., species) represents results from a single analysis/model.'
make_abundance_table(tbl_abundance_all_summary)

```
